values.value:
# How DH generates invoices
Delivery Hub generates invoices automatically on a schedule. The DeliveryInvoiceGenerationService is the entry point — it runs daily and:
1. Reads org-wide DeliveryHubSettings. If EnableInvoiceGenerationDateTime is null, the job exits silently — invoice generation is opt-in per tenant. 2. Queries every active NetworkEntity where EnableBillingDateTime is populated (the per-vendor opt-in flag). 3. For each entity, derives the period from BillingFrequencyPk: Daily covers yesterday, Weekly covers the previous Mon–Sun, Monthly covers the previous calendar month, Quarterly covers the previous calendar quarter. An On_Demand entity is skipped entirely — you generate those manually. 4. For non-Daily frequencies, the job checks whether today is the right day to run: Monthly runs only on the 1st, Quarterly only on Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct 1st. 5. Confirms at least one Approved (or null-status) WorkLog exists in the period — no hours, no invoice. 6. Delegates to DeliveryDocumentController.generateDocument, passing the period start and end. That helper picks the Invoice template for non-Daily frequencies and a Daily_Hours_Summary template for Daily. 7. Stamps LastInvoiceGenerationDate to today on the settings record so the job will not re-run the same day.
Errors on individual entities are logged and swallowed — one bad vendor cannot block the others.
Read DeliveryInvoiceGenerationService.cls for the full flow.
